title.en: DecompRL: Solving Harder Problems by Learning Modular Code Generation
title.zh: DecompRL：通过学习模块化代码生成解决更难的问题

abstract: How can Large Language Models (LLMs) solve problems they currently cannot? Repeated sampling scales test-time compute but GPU cost grows linearly with attempts, while reinforcement learning (RL) with verifiable rewards improves single-attempt accuracy at the expense of sample diversity. Both strategies ultimately fail when the base policy has near-zero probability of producing a correct solution: no amount of sampling or gradient signal can overcome a search space that is simply too large. We take a different approach: rather than sampling harder, we make the task easier by decomposing problems into smaller, independently solvable sub-functions whose implementations can be recombined. Since off-the-shelf models are not trained for this modular generation, we introduce DecompRL, an RL algorithm that explicitly learns to decompose and implement hierarchical code structures. Recombining $k$ implementations of $n$ modules yields up to $k^{n}$ candidate solutions, shifting the bottleneck from GPU inference to cheap CPU evaluation and cutting GPU token cost by $\sim$50$\times$. On LiveCodeBench and CodeContests (Qwen~2.5~7B, Code World Model~32B), DecompRL outperforms standard and diversity-optimized RL baselines beyond $10^5$ tokens per problem, solving problems that standard generation cannot reach.

relevance.en: DecompRL directly connects to your interest in RL with execution feedback for code: it learns a hierarchical decomposition policy that recombines sub‑functions, enabling RL to solve problems standard post‑training cannot reach while cutting GPU cost ~50×, thus extending execution‑based RL to harder repository‑level tasks.
relevance.zh: DecompRL直接与你对带有执行反馈的RL在代码生成中的兴趣相关：它学习了一种层次化分解策略，重组子函数，使RL能够解决标准后训练无法达到的问题，同时将GPU成本降低约50倍，从而将基于执行的RL扩展到更难的仓库级任务。

figures.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
Decomp RL solves harder problems than existing RL methods. Our hierarchical training learns 1) a decomposition
policy that breaks down problems into simpler functions and 2) an implementation policy that writes code for each
function in parallel. Given a high token budget, Decomp RL solves up to 35% problems of the LiveCodeBench hard
subset (2024/08/01 to 2025/02/01) Jain et al. (2024a).
caption.zh: Decomp RL 解决了比现有 RL 方法更困难的问题。我们的层次化训练学习了 1) 将问题分解为更简单函数的分解策略和 2) 为每个函数并行编写代码的实现策略。在高 token 预算下，Decomp RL 解决了 LiveCodeBench hard 子集（2024/08/01 至 2025/02/01）Jain 等人 (2024a) 中多达 35% 的问题。

figures.reference_text:
In practice where we consider code generation tasks in Python, we represent decompositions D by a set of functions specified by their signatures and natural language specifications in the form of docstrings. The parts Ii are then implementations of those functions. We give an example of a decomposition in Figure 1 and of a full hierarchical trajectory (D, I1, I2) in the Appendix L. We use prompted and trained language models for the decomposition and implementation policies π(D) and π(Ii | D) (see Appendix I for full prompts).

DecompRL through recombination and diverse evaluations solves new problems that are harder (Figure 1) and only solvable at high inference budgets (Figure 4 and Table 1). Using different models with DecompRL training, we beat the best existing methods for reinforcement learning when using high inference budgets (up to 3 million tokens per problem). We evaluate m = 4096 combinations per decomposition, which is roughly 80 times more than nk + 1 language model calls would have produced with standard whole-code generation. On the other hand, m = 4096 is small compared to the theoretical maximum knmax = 86 of combinations that hierarchical inference can produce. Additional CPU execution resources could further increase the appeal of the method according to the scaling behavior in Figure 4. DecompRL can help overcome the limits of our SFT checkpoint and find solutions not available with regular inference. We see it as a useful way to gather offline data to im

caption.en:
Learning to scale CPU budget. (left) Example problem from LiveCodeBench and the generated solutions with
regular and hierarchical inference. (right) Solving a problem directly in M tries leads to M complete code solutions to
evaluate. Hierarchical inference instead generates N functions each implemented K times independently. Recombining
these partial code blocks gives up to KN full code solutions to evaluate.
caption.zh: 学习扩展 CPU 预算。（左）来自 LiveCodeBench 的示例问题以及使用常规和层次化推理生成的解决方案。（右）直接在 M 次尝试中解决问题会产生 M 个完整的代码解决方案进行评估。层次化推理则生成 N 个函数，每个独立实现 K 次。重组这些部分代码块可以得到最多 KN 个完整的代码解决方案进行评估。

figures.label: Figure 3

caption.en:
Nonlinear reward transformations with utility functions. Reward transformation induced by logmeanexpβ
for different values of β, alongside the ones of pass@k: u(p) = 1 −(1 −p)k, and the mean of pass@k values. See
Appendix D for details.
caption.zh: 带有效用函数的非线性奖励变换。不同β值下的logmeanexpβ引起的奖励变换，以及pass@k的变换：u(p) = 1 − (1 − p)^k，以及pass@k值的均值。详见附录D。

figures.reference_text: This function smoothly interpolates between the average (β →∞) and the maximum (β →0), and for a well-chosen β closely approximates a log-uniform mixture of pass@k objectives (see Figure 3), balancing exploration and exploitation (see Appendix C).

caption.en:
Learning to scale CPU budget. (left) Example problem from LiveCodeBench and the generated solutions with
regular and hierarchical inference. (right) Solving a problem directly in M tries leads to M complete code solutions to
evaluate. Hierarchical inference instead generates N functions each implemented K times independently. Recombining
these partial code blocks gives up to KN full code solutions to evaluate.
caption.zh: 学习扩展 CPU 预算。（左）来自 LiveCodeBench 的示例问题以及使用常规和层次化推理生成的解决方案。（右）直接在 M 次尝试中解决问题会产生 M 个完整的代码解决方案进行评估。层次化推理则生成 N 个函数，每个独立实现 K 次。重组这些部分代码块可以得到最多 KN 个完整的代码解决方案进行评估。

brief_summary.en:
Large language models (LLMs) for code generation fail when the base policy has near-zero probability of producing a correct answer—repeated sampling and reinforcement learning (RL) both scale poorly because the search space remains too vast and undirected. DecompRL solves this by learning to decompose problems into smaller, independently solvable sub-functions and then recombining multiple implementations of each module. Because k implementations of n modules yield up to kⁿ candidate solutions, the bottleneck shifts from expensive GPU inference to cheap CPU evaluation.

DecompRL trains two separate policies via RL: a decomposition policy breaks the problem into function signatures, and an implementation policy generates multiple variants for each. Rewards are assigned using a logmeanexp of pass/fail outcomes over all recombined solutions, which balances exploration and exploitation. On LiveCodeBench hard with Qwen 2.5 7B, DecompRL reaches a 35% solve rate at ~3M tokens per problem—nearly double the best baseline—while cutting GPU token cost by roughly 50× compared to repeated sampling. This approach turns execution-based feedback into a powerful lever even when the model initially cannot generate a correct monolithic solution, making hard programming tasks tractable through learned modularity.
brief_summary.zh:
大语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成中，当基础策略生成正确答案的概率趋近于零时会失败——重复采样和强化学习（RL）都因搜索空间过大且缺乏方向性而扩展性差。DecompRL通过学习将问题分解为更小、可独立解决的子函数，然后将每个模块的多个实现重新组合来解决这一问题。由于n个模块各k个实现可产生最多kⁿ个候选解，瓶颈从昂贵的GPU推理转移到了廉价的CPU评估。

DecompRL通过RL训练两个独立的策略：分解策略将问题拆解为函数签名，实现策略为每个函数生成多个变体。奖励通过所有重组解上通过/失败结果的logmeanexp分配，平衡探索与利用。在带有Qwen 2.5 7B的LiveCodeBench hard上，DecompRL在每个问题约3M token时达到35%的解决率——几乎是最好基线的两倍——同时与重复采样相比，GPU token成本降低约50倍。这种方法将基于执行的反馈转化为强大杠杆，即使模型最初无法生成正确的整体解决方案，也能通过习得的模块化使困难的编程任务变得易处理。

problem.en: Large Language Models (LLMs) for code generation face a fundamental scalability bottleneck: when the base policy has near-zero probability of producing a correct solution, repeated sampling (test-time compute) incurs linearly growing GPU cost, while reinforcement learning (RL) with verifiable rewards improves single-attempt accuracy at the expense of sample diversity, ultimately failing to expand the search space meaningfully. Neither strategy addresses the root issue that the search space is simply too large for monolithic generation. Prior work lacks mechanisms to systematically decompose problems into smaller, independently solvable sub-tasks, leaving hard problems unsolvable even with high inference budgets. This gap is practically significant because many real-world programming tasks exhibit hierarchical structure that could be exploited, yet off-the-shelf models are not trained to produce modular code, and no existing RL framework explicitly learns decomposition and recombination together.
problem.zh: 用于代码生成的大语言模型（LLMs）面临一个根本的可扩展性瓶颈：当基础策略生成正确解的概率趋近于零时，重复采样（测试时计算）会导致线性增长的GPU成本，而带有可验证奖励的强化学习（RL）在牺牲样本多样性的情况下提高了单次尝试的准确性，最终无法有意义地扩展搜索空间。这两种策略都没有解决根本问题，即搜索空间对于整体生成来说太大。先前的工作缺乏系统地将问题分解为更小、可独立解决的子任务的机制，使得即使有高推理预算也难以解决困难问题。这一空白具有实际意义，因为许多现实世界的编程任务表现出可以利用的层次结构，然而现成的模型并未训练成生成模块化代码，也没有现有的RL框架明确地一起学习分解和重组。

solution.en: DecompRL introduces a modular code generation framework that explicitly learns to decompose problems into simpler sub-functions whose implementations can be recombined. The key insight is that generating n modules each with k independent implementations yields up to k^n candidate solutions via cheap CPU evaluation, shifting the bottleneck from expensive GPU inference to run-time execution. This is achieved by training two separate policies via reinforcement learning: a decomposition policy that breaks a problem into function signatures with docstrings, and an implementation policy that produces multiple implementations for each function. Unlike prior RL methods that optimize for single-shot success or diversity in sampled outputs, DecompRL’s hierarchical recombination enables solving problems that are effectively unsolvable by standard generation, reducing GPU token cost by approximately 50× relative to repeated sampling with equivalent coverage of the solution space.
solution.zh: DecompRL引入了一个模块化代码生成框架，明确学习将问题分解为更简单的子函数，其实现可以重组。关键见解是，生成n个模块，每个模块有k个独立实现，通过廉价的CPU评估可产生最多k^n个候选解，将瓶颈从昂贵的GPU推理转移到运行时执行。这是通过强化学习训练两个独立策略实现的：一个分解策略将问题拆解为带有文档字符串的函数签名，以及一个实现策略为每个函数生成多个实现。与先前优化单次成功或样本输出多样性的RL方法不同，DecompRL的层次化重组能够解决标准生成实际上无法解决的问题，相比于具有等效解空间覆盖的重复采样，将GPU token成本降低约50倍。

methodology.en: DecompRL operates over two learned policies: a decomposition policy π_dec and an implementation policy π_imp, both initialized from a standard instruction-tuned LLM (Qwen 2.5 7B or Code World Model 32B). For a given problem, π_dec generates a set of n function definitions (signature + docstring) forming a decomposition D. Then, π_imp independently produces k implementations for each of the n functions (sampled in parallel). During evaluation, implementations are recombined to form up to k^n complete programs, which are executed against test cases to obtain binary pass/fail outcomes. RL training alternates between updating π_imp and π_dec using a novel reward assignment mechanism: for each implementation of a particular function, its reward is computed as the logmeanexp (with temperature β) over the pass outcomes of all recombined solutions that use that implementation. The logmeanexp smoothly interpolates between mean reward (β→∞) and max reward (β→0), and is designed to approximate a log-uniform mixture of pass@k objectives, balancing exploration and exploitation. For π_dec, the reward is the logmeanexp over all recombined solutions’ outcomes for that decomposition. Advantage is estimated using a leave-one-out baseline within each group of samples. To stabilize training, the two policies are updated sequentially: one policy is frozen while the other is trained, preventing reward collapse. Key hyperparameters include β (tuned to roughly 0.5), number of implementations k (e.g., 8–16), number of decompositions sampled per problem (e.g., 1–4), and the recombination budget m (up to 4096 combinations per decomposition). The training loop collects rollouts from both policies, computes rewards via CPU evaluation, and performs gradient updates on each policy alternately over multiple iterations.
methodology.zh: DecompRL操作两个学习到的策略：分解策略π_dec和实现策略π_imp，两者都从标准的指令微调LLM（Qwen 2.5 7B或Code World Model 32B）初始化。对于给定问题，π_dec生成一组n个函数定义（签名+文档字符串），形成分解D。然后，π_imp独立地为n个函数中的每一个生成k个实现（并行采样）。在评估期间，实现被重组以形成最多k^n个完整程序，这些程序针对测试用例执行以获得二元通过/失败结果。RL训练交替更新π_imp和π_dec，使用一种新颖的奖励分配机制：对于特定函数的每个实现，其奖励计算为所有使用该实现的重组解通过结果的logmeanexp（带温度β）。logmeanexp在平均奖励（β→∞）和最大奖励（β→0）之间平滑插值，旨在近似于pass@k目标的对数均匀混合，平衡探索与利用。对于π_dec，奖励是该分解的所有重组解结果的logmeanexp。优势使用每组样本内的留一基线进行估计。为了稳定训练，两个策略顺序更新：一个策略被冻结，另一个被训练，防止奖励崩溃。关键超参数包括β（调优至大约0.5）、实现数量k（例如8–16）、每个问题采样的分解数量（例如1–4）以及重组预算m（每个分解最多4096种组合）。训练循环从两个策略收集轨迹，通过CPU评估计算奖励，并在多次迭代中交替对每个策略执行梯度更新。

experiments.en: DecompRL is evaluated on LiveCodeBench hard subset (2024/08/01 to 2025/02/01) and CodeContests validation set. Baselines include: (i) the base instruction-tuned model with repeated sampling, (ii) GRPO (standard RL for code), (iii) pass@k training (training to optimize pass@k directly), and (iv) SPO (diversity-optimized RL). Metrics are solve rate vs. total tokens per problem (inclusive of sampling and evaluation). On LiveCodeBench hard, DecompRL with Qwen 2.5 7B achieves 35% solve rate at ~3M tokens per problem, compared to ≤20% for all baselines at the same budget; the gap emerges beyond 10^5 tokens, where DecompRL continues to improve while baselines plateau. On CodeContests, DecompRL with m=4096 combinations per decomposition matches or exceeds SPO and pass@k training in pass@k for large k, while GRPO degrades pass@k despite improving pass@1. Ablations (with Llama 3.1 8B Instruct) show: (a) training both policies outperforms training only the implementation policy; (b) joint (simultaneous) training of both policies leads to entropy collapse, while sequential training is stable; (c) the logmeanexp reward transformation is crucial — replacing it with mean aggregation causes reward collapse; (d) success rate at high attempt budgets correlates with decomposition size (number of functions), not with success rate at low budgets. DecompRL reduces GPU token cost by roughly 50× compared to repeated sampling to achieve the same empirical coverage.
experiments.zh: DecompRL在LiveCodeBench hard子集（2024/08/01至2025/02/01）和CodeContests验证集上进行评估。基线包括：(i) 带有重复采样的基础指令微调模型，(ii) GRPO（用于代码的标准RL），(iii) pass@k训练（直接优化pass@k的训练），以及 (iv) SPO（多样性优化的RL）。指标是解决率与每个问题的总token数（包括采样和评估）。在LiveCodeBench hard上，使用Qwen 2.5 7B的DecompRL在每个问题约3M token时达到35%的解决率，相比之下，所有基线在相同预算下≤20%；差距在超过10^5 token时出现，DecompRL持续改进而基线趋于平稳。在CodeContests上，使用m=4096种每个分解组合的DecompRL在pass@k上对大k达到或超过SPO和pass@k训练，而GRPO虽然提高了pass@1却降低了pass@k。消融实验（使用Llama 3.1 8B Instruct）显示：(a) 训练两个策略优于仅训练实现策略；(b) 两个策略的联合（同时）训练导致熵崩溃，而顺序训练稳定；(c) logmeanexp奖励转换至关重要——用均值聚合替代会导致奖励崩溃；(d) 高尝试预算下的成功率与分解规模（函数数量）相关，与低预算下的成功率无关。DecompRL将GPU token成本降低约50倍，相比于重复采样以达到相同经验覆盖。

conclusion.en: DecompRL demonstrates that using reinforcement learning to learn modular decomposition and recombination enables solving problems that are infeasible for monolithic generation, achieving up to 50× GPU token savings by shifting the bottleneck from GPU inference to cheap CPU evaluation. This combinatorial expansion of the solution space makes execution-based feedback effective even when the base policy has near-zero correct probability, a key limitation of standard sampling. The approach relies on problems with independent sub-functions and test cases, and does not extend to repository-level code with cross-file dependencies. Overall, it suggests a promising direction for making problem difficulty learnable and for incorporating hierarchical structure into code generation.
conclusion.zh: DecompRL证明，使用强化学习学习模块化分解和重组能够解决整体生成不可行的问题，通过将瓶颈从GPU推理转移到廉价的CPU评估，实现了高达50倍的GPU token节省。这种解空间的组合扩展使得基于执行的反馈在基础策略正确概率趋近于零时仍然有效，这是标准采样的一个关键限制。该方法依赖于具有独立子函数和测试用例的问题，并不扩展到具有跨文件依赖的仓库级代码。总体而言，它为让问题难度可学习和将层次结构纳入代码生成指明了一个有希望的方向。

question.en: How does DecompRL's use of reinforcement learning with execution-based rewards compare to preference learning methods like RLHF for improving code generation correctness on complex tasks?
question.zh: 在复杂的代码生成任务中，DecompRL使用基于执行奖励的强化学习与RLHF等偏好学习方法相比，在提高代码生成正确性方面如何？

reason.en: The user is interested in post-training techniques (RLHF, RL with execution feedback) that improve code correctness. This question directly compares DecompRL's RL approach with preference-based methods, probing the paper's methodology and results.
reason.zh: 用户对改进代码正确性的后训练技术（RLHF、带有执行反馈的RL）感兴趣。这个问题直接比较了DecompRL的RL方法与基于偏好的方法，探究论文的方法和结果。

question.en: Could DecompRL's modular decomposition and recombination approach be extended to repository-level code generation scenarios, and what modifications would be needed to handle cross-file dependencies?
question.zh: DecompRL的模块化分解与重组方法能否扩展到仓库级代码生成场景？处理跨文件依赖需要哪些修改？

reason.en: The user's focus includes repository-level generation and long-context handling. This question explores the scalability of DecompRL beyond single-problem settings to repository-level tasks, directly addressing a key interest.
reason.zh: 用户的关注点包括仓库级生成和长上下文处理。这个问题探讨了DecompRL从单问题设置扩展到仓库级任务的可扩展性，直接回应用户的一个关键兴趣。

question.en: What novel evaluation metrics does DecompRL's recombination framework suggest beyond pass@k, and how do they better capture the efficiency and scalability of code generation?
question.zh: DecompRL的重组框架在pass@k之外提出了哪些新颖的评估指标？它们如何更好地捕捉代码生成的效率和可扩展性？

reason.en: The user cares about evaluation methodologies beyond pass@k. This question probes how DecompRL's focus on GPU token cost and recombination efficiency can lead to new metrics, aligning with the user's interest in realistic evaluation.
reason.zh: 用户关注pass@k之外的评估方法。这个问题探究了DecompRL对GPU token成本和重组效率的关注如何引出新指标，与用户对现实评估的兴趣一致。

question.en: How does learning to decompose problems into sub-functions via RL enable solving problems that are infeasible for monolithic generation, and what implications does this have for post-training with execution feedback?
question.zh: 通过学习将问题分解为子函数，RL如何使解决那些整体生成不可行的问题成为可能？这对基于执行反馈的后训练有何启示？

reason.en: This question dives into the core contribution of the paper—learning decomposition—and connects it to the user's interest in post-training with execution feedback, asking for insights on the mechanism and broader applicability.
reason.zh: 这个问题深入探讨了论文的核心贡献——学习分解——并将其与用户对带有执行反馈的后训练的兴趣联系起来，询问机制和更广泛适用性的见解。

question.en: How does DecompRL's use of reinforcement learning with execution-based rewards compare to preference learning methods like RLHF for improving code generation correctness on complex tasks?
question.zh: 在复杂的代码生成任务中，DecompRL使用基于执行奖励的强化学习与RLHF等偏好学习方法相比，在提高代码生成正确性方面如何？

reason.en: The user is interested in post-training techniques (RLHF, RL with execution feedback) that improve code correctness. This question directly compares DecompRL's RL approach with preference-based methods, probing the paper's methodology and results.
reason.zh: 用户对改进代码正确性的后训练技术（RLHF、带有执行反馈的RL）感兴趣。这个问题直接比较了DecompRL的RL方法与基于偏好的方法，探究论文的方法和结果。

answer.en: The paper does not compare DecompRL to preference learning methods like RLHF. It focuses on RL with execution-based rewards, noting that 'reinforcement learning (RL) with verifiable rewards improves single-attempt accuracy' but does not discuss RLHF. No direct comparison is made.
answer.zh: 论文没有将DecompRL与RLHF等偏好学习方法进行比较。它专注于基于执行奖励的RL，指出“带有可验证奖励的强化学习（RL）提高了单次尝试的准确性”，但没有讨论RLHF。未进行直接比较。

question.en: Could DecompRL's modular decomposition and recombination approach be extended to repository-level code generation scenarios, and what modifications would be needed to handle cross-file dependencies?
question.zh: DecompRL的模块化分解与重组方法能否扩展到仓库级代码生成场景？处理跨文件依赖需要哪些修改？

reason.en: The user's focus includes repository-level generation and long-context handling. This question explores the scalability of DecompRL beyond single-problem settings to repository-level tasks, directly addressing a key interest.
reason.zh: 用户的关注点包括仓库级生成和长上下文处理。这个问题探讨了DecompRL从单问题设置扩展到仓库级任务的可扩展性，直接回应用户的一个关键兴趣。

answer.en: The paper does not address repository-level code generation or cross-file dependencies. DecompRL focuses on decomposing individual problems into independently solvable sub-functions (abstract: 'decomposing problems into smaller, independently solvable sub-functions'). The modular recombination assumes modules are independent, so cross-file dependencies would break that assumption. No modifications for such scenarios are discussed.
answer.zh: 论文没有涉及仓库级代码生成或跨文件依赖。DecompRL专注于将单个问题分解为可独立解决的子函数（摘要：“将问题分解为更小、可独立解决的子函数”）。模块化重组假设模块是独立的，因此跨文件依赖会打破这一假设。没有讨论针对此类场景的修改。

question.en: What novel evaluation metrics does DecompRL's recombination framework suggest beyond pass@k, and how do they better capture the efficiency and scalability of code generation?
question.zh: DecompRL的重组框架在pass@k之外提出了哪些新颖的评估指标？它们如何更好地捕捉代码生成的效率和可扩展性？

reason.en: The user cares about evaluation methodologies beyond pass@k. This question probes how DecompRL's focus on GPU token cost and recombination efficiency can lead to new metrics, aligning with the user's interest in realistic evaluation.
reason.zh: 用户关注pass@k之外的评估方法。这个问题探究了DecompRL对GPU token成本和重组效率的关注如何引出新指标，与用户对现实评估的兴趣一致。

answer.en: The paper does not explicitly propose new evaluation metrics beyond pass@k, but its recombination framework motivates two key metrics: GPU token cost and recombination efficiency (e.g., candidate solutions per GPU token). The abstract states that "recombining k implementations of n modules yields up to k^n candidate solutions, shifting the bottleneck from GPU inference to cheap CPU evaluation and cutting GPU token cost by ~50×." This directly contrasts with pass@k, which counts successful solutions among k samples but ignores the quadratic GPU cost of sampling. DecompRL's metrics better capture real-world efficiency and scalability because they measure the cost to generate many candidates, not just the success rate at a fixed budget. The paper reports performance "beyond 10^5 tokens per problem," emphasizing that GPU token cost, not sample count, is the limiting factor in practice.
answer.zh: 论文没有明确提出pass@k之外的新评估指标，但其重组框架推动了两个关键指标：GPU token成本和重组效率（例如，每个GPU token的候选解数量）。摘要指出，“重组n个模块各k个实现可产生最多k^n个候选解，将瓶颈从GPU推理转移到廉价的CPU评估，并将GPU token成本降低约50倍。”这与pass@k形成直接对比，pass@k统计k个样本中的成功解数量，但忽略了采样的二次GPU成本。DecompRL的指标更好地捕捉了现实世界的效率和可扩展性，因为它们衡量生成多个候选解的成本，而不仅仅是固定预算下的成功率。论文报告了性能“在每个问题超过10^5 token”，强调了实际中限制因素是GPU token成本，而不是样本数量。

question.en: How does learning to decompose problems into sub-functions via RL enable solving problems that are infeasible for monolithic generation, and what implications does this have for post-training with execution feedback?
question.zh: 通过学习将问题分解为子函数，RL如何使解决那些整体生成不可行的问题成为可能？这对基于执行反馈的后训练有何启示？

reason.en: This question dives into the core contribution of the paper—learning decomposition—and connects it to the user's interest in post-training with execution feedback, asking for insights on the mechanism and broader applicability.
reason.zh: 这个问题深入探讨了论文的核心贡献——学习分解——并将其与用户对带有执行反馈的后训练的兴趣联系起来，询问机制和更广泛适用性的见解。

answer.en: Learning to decompose problems into sub-functions via reinforcement learning (RL) enables solving problems infeasible for monolithic generation by expanding the solution space combinatorially without proportional GPU cost. As the abstract states, monolithic approaches 'ultimately fail when the base policy has near-zero probability of producing a correct solution: no amount of sampling or gradient signal can overcome a search space that is simply too large.' DecompRL addresses this by 'decomposing problems into smaller, independently solvable sub-functions whose implementations can be recombined,' achieving up to 'k^{n} candidate solutions' while 'shifting the bottleneck from GPU inference to cheap CPU evaluation and cutting GPU token cost by ∼50×.' This directly implies that post-training with execution feedback can be made far more efficient and effective by incorporating decomposition into the RL objective: the algorithm learns to produce modular code that can be recombined, outperforming 'standard and diversity-optimized RL baselines beyond 10^5 tokens per problem' and 'solving problems that standard generation cannot reach.' The combinatorial boost from recombination thus makes execution feedback a viable signal even when the base policy has near-zero correct probability.
answer.zh: 通过强化学习（RL）学习将问题分解为子函数，能够以组合方式扩展解空间而无需成比例的GPU成本，从而解决整体生成不可行的问题。正如摘要所述，整体方法“在基础策略生成正确解的概率趋近于零时最终失败：任何数量的采样或梯度信号都无法克服过大的搜索空间。”DecompRL通过“将问题分解为更小、可独立解决的子函数，其实现可以重组”来解决这个问题，达到“最多k^n个候选解”，同时“将瓶颈从GPU推理转移到廉价的CPU评估，并将GPU token成本降低约50倍。”这直接表明，通过将分解纳入RL目标，可以使基于执行反馈的后训练更加高效和有效：算法学会生成可重组的模块化代码，超越“每个问题超过10^5 token的标准和多样性优化的RL基线”，并“解决标准生成无法达到的问题”。因此，重组的组合提升使得执行反馈在基础策略正确概率趋近于零时也能成为可行的信号。
